```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  a <- sample(2:6,1); y <- runif(1,0.2*a^2,0.8*a^2)
  ans <- 1/(2*a*sqrt(y))
  vals <- round(c(ans,1/(a*sqrt(y)),sqrt(y)/a,1/(2*a*y),2*sqrt(y)/a),5)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.5f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $X\sim U(0,`r a`)$ e $Y=X^2$, qual é $f_Y(`r sprintf("%.3f",y)`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A inversa é $x=\sqrt y$ e $|dx/dy|=1/(2\sqrt y)$. Assim,
$$f_Y(y)=\frac1{`r a`}\frac1{2\sqrt y},
\quad 0<y<`r a^2`,$$
que no ponto dado vale `r sprintf("%.5f",ans)`.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S7Q6_transformacao_quadrado_monotona
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
